name: Crisp German Meatballs
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine sausage and onion in a large pan; cook until sausage is browned, stirring to crumble it.
2. Drain well. Stir in sauerkraut and 2 tablespoons breadcrumbs.
3. In a large bowl, combine cream cheese and the next four ingredients (parsley, prepared mustard, garlic salt, and pepper); add sausage mixture, stirring well. Cover and let stand for 2 hours.
4. Combine mayo and prepared mustard; set aside.
5. In a small bowl, combine eggs and milk; set aside.
6. Shape sausage mixture into 3/4-inch balls; roll each ball in flour.
7. Dip each ball first in the egg mixture, then roll in breadcrumbs.
8. Heat oil to a depth of 2 inches in a deep skillet or Dutch oven to 375°F (190°C).
9. Fry meatballs, a few at a time, for 2 minutes or until golden brown.
10. Drain on paper towels.
11. Serve with the mayo and mustard mixture. Yields about 5 dozen.
